name: Hot Springs
title: Hot Springs, Arkansas — A Living Chart
desc: Bathhouse Row and the Ouachita ridges in five switchable editions.
cardDesc: America's first resort town — thermal water, mountain ridges and lake country in five editions.
eyebrow: United States · Ouachitas · Arkansas
tagline:
Thermal water & mountain ridges
in the oldest national park town
about: Hot Springs grew up around the 143-degree springs at the foot of Hot Springs Mountain — a federal reservation since 1832, four decades before Yellowstone. Bathhouse Row remains the grandest line of bathhouses in America.
Around the town the Ouachita ridges run east to west, with the tower on Hot Springs Mountain, the lakes — Hamilton, Catherine, Ouachita — and trail networks in every direction.
terrainNote: The Ouachitas are old folded sandstone running east–west — the ridgelines and the valleys between them organize everything here. Drag with the right mouse button (or two fingers) to tilt and turn the map.
cartouche: A LIVING CHART OF HOT SPRINGS

landmarks.name: Bathhouse Row
landmarks.blurb: Eight grand bathhouses from the golden age of taking the waters, now the heart of the national park.

landmarks.name: Hot Springs Mountain Tower
landmarks.blurb: A 216-foot tower on the mountaintop above the Row — the view runs the whole length of the Ouachitas.
